name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday loans ($100–$500), installment loans ($100–$1,000), and title loans ($2,000–$25,000) with same-day funding at physical locations and online.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized lender operating physical storefronts across multiple states, including the Jacksonville, FL location at 1020-14 N. Edgewood Ave. The company specializes in short-term emergency cash solutions designed to bridge income gaps between paychecks or cover unexpected expenses. With over 125,459 customer reviews averaging 4.9 out of 5 stars, Advance America positions itself as a trusted alternative for consumers who need fast access to cash.

The company offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans (2–4 week terms, $100–$500), Installment Loans (flexible repayment over time, $100–$1,000), and Title Loans (collateral-based loans using vehicle equity, $2,000–$25,000). All products are available both online and at physical store locations. The Jacksonville store maintains extended hours (9 am–6 pm weekdays, 9 am–4 pm Saturday, closed Sunday) and provides bilingual Spanish-language support. The application process requires government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN.

Advance America differentiates itself through same-day funding availability, multiple loan product options at varying amounts, and physical storefront accessibility for in-person consultations. The company also offers Western Union services and operates a referral rewards program. The Jacksonville location specifically serves the Commonwealth Village area between I-95 and I-295, positioning itself for local walk-in traffic and phone-based applications.

However, this company operates in the high-cost emergency lending space. Payday and installment loans typically carry APRs well above 36%, making them substantially more expensive than traditional personal loans or payday-alternative programs. The company does not disclose specific APR, fee structures, or rollover policies on its website. While customer ratings are strong, the absence of transparent pricing information and the inherent cost structure of short-term lending mean this service is best suited only for genuine emergencies when no other options are available.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding available online and in-store for all loan types
Loan amounts scale from $100–$25,000 depending on product, accommodating different emergency levels
Extended store hours (9 am–6 pm weekdays, 9 am–4 pm Saturday) provide accessibility for working customers
Bilingual Spanish-language customer service at physical locations
4.9-star average rating across 125,459+ customer reviews demonstrates consistent user satisfaction
Multiple loan products (payday, installment, title) allow borrowers to choose repayment flexibility
No prepayment penalties mentioned; installment loans offer multi-payment repayment terms vs. lump-sum payday loans
cons: APR, interest rates, and fee schedules are not disclosed on the website, preventing cost transparency
Payday and installment loans typically carry APRs significantly above 36%, making them substantially more expensive than alternatives
Title loans require the vehicle to be fully paid off and registered in the borrower's name, limiting accessibility
No information provided about rollover policies, default consequences, or debt spiral protections
Physical location is only in Jacksonville; online applications may be limited to certain states or require partner companies (e.g., LoanCenter for title loans)
best_for: Workers facing a genuine short-term cash emergency with no other funding options (medical bills, car repairs, eviction risk)
Borrowers with vehicles paid in full who need larger emergency amounts ($2,000+) and can afford title loan interest costs
Customers with poor credit or limited credit history who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or bank credit
Bilingual Spanish-speakers in the Jacksonville area who prefer in-person loan consultation and processing
diagnosis: Advance America is best for consumers facing genuine short-term cash emergencies who lack alternative funding sources and can afford high-cost borrowing. The primary caveat is the absence of transparent APR and fee disclosure on the website—payday and installment loans in this category typically carry 300%+ APRs, and borrowers should verify exact costs before committing. This product is a last-resort emergency solution, not a long-term financial strategy.
services: Title loans ($2,000–$25,000 collateral-based loans using vehicle equity)
In-store loan applications and same-day funding
Physical storefront locations with extended hours

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
